mcqs.question: What is the primary goal of RISC architecture?

options.text: To increase the number of instructions
To reduce the number of instructions
To improve code density
To simplify compiler design

mcqs.explanation: RISC architecture aims to reduce the number of instructions to improve execution speed

mcqs.question: What is the primary advantage of CISC architecture?

options.text: Faster execution speed
Improved code density
Simpler compiler design
Reduced memory usage

mcqs.explanation: CISC architecture improves code density by increasing the number of instructions

mcqs.question: What is pipelining in RISC architecture?

options.text: A technique to improve code density
A technique to simplify compiler design
A technique to improve execution speed
A technique to reduce memory usage

mcqs.explanation: Pipelining is a technique to improve execution speed by breaking down instructions into stages

mcqs.question: What is microcode in CISC architecture?

options.text: A set of high-level instructions
A set of low-level instructions
A set of complex instructions
A set of simple instructions

mcqs.explanation: Microcode is a set of low-level instructions that implement complex instructions

mcqs.question: What is the difference between RISC and CISC instruction sets?

options.text: RISC has a larger instruction set
CISC has a larger instruction set
RISC has a simpler instruction set
CISC has a simpler instruction set

mcqs.explanation: CISC has a larger instruction set with more complex instructions
